@srcgroup • 22m ago

7:30PM CFB: Miami Vs. Indiana (CFB POY)

Picks:
IU -6.5 -142 25U (FD) POY
IU TTO 26.5 -138 5U (FD)
MIA TTU 20.5 -148 5U (FD)
IU 1H -3.5 -130 3U (DK)
IU Alt -29.5 +1000 1U (FD)

7:30PM CFB: Miami vs. Indiana

As Miami and Indiana get set to meet in the National Championship, it is hard to believe that this is the first ever championship game in football. The first seed has fully lived up to expectations in the postseason, beating Alabama and Clemson by a combined 42 points in the playoff games. On the other side, Indiana has looked dominant from start to finish on both sides of the ball. On the offensive side, Miami entered the postseason averaging 44.5 points per game, with a balanced attack that saw them rank 1st in the country in both passing and rushing, knocking off Texas A&M 10 to 3, Ohio State 24 to 14, and Ole Miss 31 to 27. While Miami has put together a strong postseason run, it comes to an end when Indiana shows why they are the best team in the country.

Through their first three postseason wins, Miami relied heavily on its defense to carry it to victory. They held Texas A&M to 10 points, Ohio State to 14, and Ole Miss to 27. However, Miami’s defense is a notable accomplishment against that type of offense. However, it is very unlikely this Miami defense is close to put up the same type of performance against an Indiana offense that is averaging 43 points per game while over 7 yards per play. 5.3 yards per carry, and 10.5 yards per pass attempt. It is even more impressive is that it has held up against elite defenses, as Indiana scored 34 total points against Alabama and Oregon in the playoffs.

On the other side, while Miami has managed to win each of its last three games, the Hurricanes’ offense has seen its production decline. In the last three games, Miami is averaging just 217 points per game while producing 5.1 yards per play, 4.5 yards per carry, and 6.1 yards per pass attempt. This is a far cry from the 44.5 points per game and 7.1 yards per play that they averaged in their season overages and now face an Indiana defense that continues to trend upward. Indiana is allowing just 117 points per game in the postseason, and their defense has been the key to their success. Miami’s offensive numbers are set to fall further in this spot.

Indiana has been the best team in the country all season, and that strength carried them through these strong postseason wins to reach the championship. In most seasons, that level of defensive dominance would be enough to win the championship. However, tonight is different. Indiana negates Miami’s strength by owning the most explosive offense in the country while also having the best defense in the country. Indiana is expected to win this game on both sides of the ball from the opening kick. Expect a decisive Indiana win.
